associated_files.description: The fit results in json format. The fields "fit_val", "fit_err" and "syst" represent the results before the Neyman corrections. The results after the Neyman corrections have been applied are "val" (central value) and "hi_err" and "lo_err" for the total asymmetric uncertainties. The total asymmetric uncertainties have also been split into statistical and systematic components.

Fit configuration (ii); the partially massive model with CP-asymmetries fixed to 0, and with the optimised Pi basis for the CP-averaged observables. The standard q2 binning is used. This table presents the statistical correlation matrix as obtained from the Hessian matrix of the fit. In yaml format.

Fit configuration (ii); the partially massive model with CP-asymmetries fixed to 0, and with the optimised Pi basis for the CP-averaged observables. The standard q2 binning is used. This table presents the statistical correlation matrix as obtained from the Hessian matrix of the fit. In json format from a pandas data frame.

Fit configuration (ii); the partially massive model with CP-asymmetries fixed to 0, and with the optimised Pi basis for the CP-averaged observables. The standard q2 binning is used. This table presents the correlation matrix of the systematic uncertainties, without including any uncertainties from the corrections of the Neyman construction. In yaml format.

Fit configuration (ii); the partially massive model with CP-asymmetries fixed to 0, and with the optimised Pi basis for the CP-averaged observables. The standard q2 binning is used. This table presents the correlation matrix of the systematic uncertainties, without including any uncertainties from the corrections of the Neyman construction. In json format from a pandas data frame.

Fit configuration (ii); the partially massive model with CP-asymmetries fixed to 0, and with the optimised Pi basis for the CP-averaged observables. The standard q2 binning is used. This table presents the total correlation matrix when summing the statistical and systematic covarainces, as well as the variances (both statistical and systematic) arising from the Neyman construction. When adding the Neyman consrtruction uncertainties, no change is made to the statistical correlation matrix and the systematic uncertainties from the Neyman construction are treated as uncorrelated between observables and bins. Where there is an asymmetric uncertainty, the mean of the two values is assigned. In yaml format.

Fit configuration (ii); the partially massive model with CP-asymmetries fixed to 0, and with the optimised Pi basis for the CP-averaged observables. The standard q2 binning is used. This table presents the total correlation matrix when summing the statistical and systematic covarainces, as well as the variances (both statistical and systematic) arising from the Neyman construction. When adding the Neyman consrtruction uncertainties, no change is made to the statistical correlation matrix and the systematic uncertainties from the Neyman construction are treated as uncorrelated between observables and bins. Where there is an asymmetric uncertainty, the mean of the two values is assigned. In json format from a pandas data frame.

description: Fit configuration (ii); the partially massive model with CP-asymmetries fixed to 0, and with the optimised Pi basis for the CP-averaged observables. The standard q2 binning is used. This table presents the results for all obervables in all q2 bins. The central values of the observables after (val) bias corrections are given in the yaml. Additionally in the json the results before bias corrections are provided (fit_val). The statistical uncertainty from the Hessian matrix of the fit is presented (fit_err) as well as the assigned systematic uncertainty before the correction from the Neyman construction (detector). Finally the asymmetric statistical uncertainty obtained from the Neyman construction is shown (stat) as well as the corresponding total systematic uncertainty, including that from the Neyman construction (syst) and the resulting total asymmetric uncertainties (total). Where there is no difference between the asymmetric and symmetric uncertainties and the two central values in the json there were no corrections from the Neyman construction applied.
name: config_2_results
